title: Cyclosorus oblancifolius
taxonomy: Cyclosorus oblancifolius is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It is native to tropical Asia. Cyclosorus oblancifolius is also known as a plant with divided leaves and is often found in moist environments. The classification of Cyclosorus oblancifolius is as a member of the genus Cyclosorus and the order Polypodiales. Further classification includes it being a species of fern in the division Pteridophyta.
uses: Cyclosorus oblancifolius, also known as the maiden's hair fern, is traditionally used in medicine. The plant is believed to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Its leaves are used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. Furthermore, it is also used as a natural remedy for skin conditions and wounds. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for medicinal use.
benefits: Cyclosorus oblancifolius, also known as the lady fern, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in reducing pain and inflammation in the body. constituents: Cyclosorus oblancifolius, a plant native to Asia, has been found to contain various active constituents that contribute to its potential health benefits. The active constituents of Cyclosorus oblancifolius include flavonoids, which are known for their antioxidant properties, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. preparations: Cyclosorus oblancifolius, also known as the ladder fern, has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world.
